title: Insilico's AI-designed IPF drug becomes the first end-to-end AI drug to reach Phase III
subtitle: Rentosertib — target identified by AI, molecule generated by AI, trial design guided by AI — enters a 320-patient pivotal trial for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis across 47 Chinese centers.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Insilico Medicine announced on July 7 that it has enrolled the first patient in a Phase III pivotal trial for rentosertib, an oral TNIK inhibitor for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The trial is designed to run 52 weeks across 47 Chinese centers with 320 patients randomized to placebo or once-daily rentosertib. The molecule's Phase IIa results — a mean +98.4 mL forced-vital-capacity improvement at 12 weeks in the 60 mg arm, versus a −20.3 mL decline on placebo — were published in Nature Medicine in June 2025 as the first-ever peer-reviewed proof-of-concept for AI-driven drug discovery.

The story matters because rentosertib is the first drug where the target was identified by AI, the molecule was generated by AI, and the clinical development strategy was guided by AI-based outcome prediction — and all three steps have now cleared enough validation for the compound to enter pivotal registration testing. Every prior 'AI-designed' drug in Phase III has used AI at only one step of the pipeline. Rentosertib is the first end-to-end case. If Phase III succeeds, the FDA and NMPA will have to decide how much AI-derived evidence counts inside a registration package, and pharma will have a template for how much of a drug-development pipeline can be handed to a model.

That decision matters for the roughly 175 AI-originated programs now in human trials and the $60 billion invested in the sector since 2019. Industry estimates put 15 to 20 more AI programs into Phase III this year. Recursion, Exscientia, BenevolentAI, and Isomorphic Labs are all watching whether a rentosertib readout would justify the sector's valuations or expose the same gap between preclinical promise and clinical delivery that has defined biotech for decades. The trial's endpoint reads out in 2028, which sets a hard schedule for the first real test of the thesis.

For learners: 'AI in drug discovery' is a phrase that hides an enormous range of technical claims. Some programs use machine learning to score a chemical library. Some use generative models to draw new molecules. A very small number use AI at every step from target identification to clinical trial design. Before you evaluate a headline about an 'AI drug,' ask which steps of the pipeline the AI actually did and which steps a human team ran the traditional way. That distinction — where the model helped versus where it took over — is what separates marketing from science in this field.

sources.title: Insilico Initiates Phase III Clinical Trial for Rentosertib, Its AI-Empowered TNIK Inhibitor for Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis — Insilico Medicine
